description: §6.8.2 — `reset` is a RESERVED IDENTIFIER. Declaring a local `function reset() {...}` shadows the `reset(@cell)` builtin and fires E-RESERVED-IDENTIFIER (compile error, §34). Pins the enforcement so the impl (ast-builder function/fn parse guards) cannot silently regress.
rationale: §6.8.2: declaring a local function named `reset` (either `function reset() {...}` or `fn reset {...}`) is E-RESERVED-IDENTIFIER. The `function` form is used here (a `fn` form would additionally fire E-FN-003 for the reactive write). Author must rename the handler (e.g. `clearCount`) or use the `reset(@cell)` builtin.
